response:
I don’t have access to your meeting notes, CRM, or internal docs, so I can’t list the specific customers discussed. If you tell me where the discussion happened (meeting, Confluence page, Slack thread, Salesforce opportunity, etc.) I can pull that out or give precise search steps. Meanwhile, here are practical ways to find who was discussed and quick queries you can run.

Where to look
- Project kickoff or status meeting notes (Confluence, OneNote, Google Doc)
- Email thread or calendar invite attendees for the sentinel-related meetings
- CRM (Salesforce, HubSpot) — opportunities or accounts tied to Project Sentinel
- Slack/Teams search for “Project Sentinel”, “Sentinel” or code-name variations
- Jira/Asana tickets or a project charter that may list pilot/reference customers
- Product/partner management docs (roadmaps, partner lists)
- Recording transcripts (Google Meet, Teams) — search the transcript for customer names

Quick search/query examples
- Salesforce SOQL (example): SELECT Account.Name, Opportunity.Name, Stage FROM Opportunity WHERE Project__c = 'Sentinel' OR Name LIKE '%Sentinel%'
- HubSpot filter: Filter deals by property “Project” = Sentinel OR deal name contains “Sentinel”
- Slack search: in:#project-sentinel OR from:@pm "Sentinel" OR "pilot"
- Confluence/Google Doc: search for “Project Sentinel”, “Sentinel pilot”, “Sentinel customer”, “reference”

Common categories of customers to expect in project docs
- Pilot customers (early adopters for validation)
- Reference customers (for demos/case studies)
- Strategic/enterprise accounts (priority for rollout)
- Channel partners or OEMs (if project includes partners)
- Internal customers (other business units)

If you paste the meeting notes, an excerpt, or tell me which system the discussion was in, I’ll extract the customer names and create a concise list and next actions (e.g., outreach templates, NDA checks, contact owners).
